1: "use client";
2: 
3: import { Tabs as TabsPrimitive } from "radix-ui";
4: 
5: import type { FeedbackType } from "@/hooks/use-feedback";
6: import { useFeedback } from "@/hooks/use-feedback";
7: import { cn } from "@/lib/utils";
8: 
9: const Tabs = ({
10:   className,
11:   ...props
12: }: React.ComponentProps<typeof TabsPrimitive.Root>) => (
13:   <TabsPrimitive.Root
14:     data-slot="tabs"
15:     className={cn("flex flex-col gap-2", className)}
16:     {...props}
17:   />
18: );
19: 
20: const TabsList = ({
21:   className,
22:   ...props
23: }: React.ComponentProps<typeof TabsPrimitive.List>) => (
24:   <TabsPrimitive.List
25:     data-slot="tabs-list"
26:     className={cn(
27:       "bg-muted text-muted-foreground inline-flex h-9 w-fit items-center justify-center rounded-lg p-[3px]",
28:       className
29:     )}
30:     {...props}
31:   />
32: );
33: 
34: const TabsTrigger = ({
35:   className,
36:   onClick,
37:   sound,
38:   haptic,
39:   ...props
40: }: React.ComponentProps<typeof TabsPrimitive.Trigger> & {
41:   sound?: FeedbackType;
42:   haptic?: boolean;
43: }) => {
44:   const play = useFeedback({ haptic, sound });
45: 
46:   const handleClick: React.MouseEventHandler<HTMLButtonElement> = (e) => {
47:     play();
48:     onClick?.(e);
49:   };
50: 
51:   return (
52:     <TabsPrimitive.Trigger
53:       data-slot="tabs-trigger"
54:       onClick={handleClick}
55:       className={cn(
56:         "data-[state=active]:bg-background dark:data-[state=active]:text-foreground focus-visible:border-ring focus-visible:ring-ring/50 focus-visible:outline-ring dark:data-[state=active]:border-input dark:data-[state=active]:bg-input/30 text-foreground dark:text-muted-foreground inline-flex h-[calc(100%-1px)] flex-1 items-center justify-center gap-1.5 rounded-md border border-transparent px-2 py-1 text-sm font-medium whitespace-nowrap transition-[color,box-shadow] focus-visible:ring-[3px] focus-visible:outline-1 disabled:pointer-events-none disabled:opacity-50 data-[state=active]:shadow-sm [&_svg]:pointer-events-none [&_svg]:shrink-0 [&_svg:not([class*='size-'])]:size-4",
57:         className
58:       )}
59:       {...props}
60:     />
61:   );
62: };
63: 
64: const TabsContent = ({
65:   className,
66:   ...props
67: }: React.ComponentProps<typeof TabsPrimitive.Content>) => (
68:   <TabsPrimitive.Content
69:     data-slot="tabs-content"
70:     className={cn("flex-1 outline-none", className)}
71:     {...props}
72:   />
73: );
74: 
75: export { Tabs, TabsContent, TabsList, TabsTrigger };

<path>/home/himmel/personal/prsui/content/docs/components/accordion.mdx</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: ---
2: title: Accordion
3: description: Animated accordion with spring physics, group morphing, and accessible ARIA attributes.
4: ---
5: 
6: import { BouncyAccordionPreview } from "@/demo/accordion-demo";
7: 
8: <ComponentPreview name="accordion">
9:   <BouncyAccordionPreview />
10: </ComponentPreview>
11: 
12: ## Installation
13: 
14: If you've already added the prsui registry, use the short command:
15: 
16: <CodeBlockCommand
17:   __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest add @prsui/accordion"
18:   __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add @prsui/accordion"
19:   __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @prsui/accordion"
20:   __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest add @prsui/accordion"
21: />
22: 
23: Otherwise, use the full registry URL:
24: 
25: <CodeBlockCommand
26:   __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/accordion.json"
27:   __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/accordion.json"
28:   __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/accordion.json"
29:   __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/accordion.json"
30: />
31: 
32: ## Usage
33: 
34: After you add the component with the CLI, import it from your UI folder:
35: 
36: ```tsx
37: import { Accordion, type AccordionItem } from "@/components/ui/accordion";
38: 
39: const items: AccordionItem[] = [
40:   {
41:     id: "item-1",
42:     title: "First question",
43:     description: "Answer to the first question.",
44:   },
45:   {
46:     id: "item-2",
47:     title: "Second question",
48:     description: "Answer to the second question.",
49:   },
50: ];
51: 
52: export function MyComponent() {
53:   return <Accordion items={items} defaultValue="item-1" />;
54: }
55: ```
56: 
57: ## Props
58: 
59: | Prop            | Type                              | Default | Description                                           |
60: | --------------- | --------------------------------- | ------- | ----------------------------------------------------- |
61: | `items`         | `AccordionItem[]`                 | -       | Array of accordion items                              |
62: | `value`         | `string \| null`                  | -       | Controlled currently open item id                     |
63: | `defaultValue`  | `string \| null`                  | `null`  | Initially open item id                                |
64: | `onValueChange` | `(value: string \| null) => void` | -       | Callback fired when the open item changes             |
65: | `collapsible`   | `boolean`                         | `true`  | When false, an open item cannot be closed by clicking |
66: | `className`     | `string`                          | -       | Classes applied to the root wrapper                   |
67: | `classNames`    | `AccordionClassNames`             | -       | Override classes for individual sub-elements          |
68: 
69: ### AccordionItem
70: 
71: | Field         | Type        | Description                              |
72: | ------------- | ----------- | ---------------------------------------- |
73: | `id`          | `string`    | Unique identifier for the item           |
74: | `title`       | `ReactNode` | Content shown in the trigger row         |
75: | `description` | `ReactNode` | Content revealed when the item is open   |
76: | `icon`        | `ReactNode` | Optional icon displayed before the title |
77: | `disabled`    | `boolean`   | Disables the item                        |
78: 
79: ### AccordionClassNames
80: 
81: | Key           | Description                  |
82: | ------------- | ---------------------------- |
83: | `root`        | Wrapper around all items     |
84: | `item`        | Each accordion row container |
85: | `trigger`     | The clickable button row     |
86: | `icon`        | The icon wrapper span        |
87: | `title`       | The title text span          |
88: | `chevron`     | The chevron rotation wrapper |
89: | `content`     | The animated content wrapper |
90: | `description` | The description text div     |

(End of file - total 90 lines)
</content>

<path>/home/himmel/personal/prsui/content/docs/components/editable-chip.mdx</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: ---
2: title: Editable Chip
3: description: An inline-editable chip component with animated transitions between display and edit modes.
4: ---
5: 
6: import { EditableChipPreview } from "@/demo/editable-chip-demo";
7: 
8: <ComponentPreview name="editable-chip">
9:   <EditableChipPreview />
10: </ComponentPreview>
11: 
12: ## Installation
13: 
14: If you've already added the prsui registry, use the short command:
15: 
16: <CodeBlockCommand
17:   __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest add @prsui/editable-chip"
18:   __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add @prsui/editable-chip"
19:   __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @prsui/editable-chip"
20:   __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest add @prsui/editable-chip"
21: />
22: 
23: Otherwise, use the full registry URL:
24: 
25: <CodeBlockCommand
26:   __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/editable-chip.json"
27:   __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/editable-chip.json"
28:   __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/editable-chip.json"
29:   __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/editable-chip.json"
30: />
31: 
32: ## Usage
33: 
34: After you add the component with the CLI, import it from your UI folder:
35: 
36: ```tsx
37: import { EditableChip } from "@/components/ui/editable-chip";
38: 
39: export function MyComponent() {
40:   return (
41:     <EditableChip
42:       defaultLabel="Watchlist"
43:       onChange={(value) => console.log(value)}
44:     />
45:   );
46: }
47: ```
48: 
49: ## Props
50: 
51: | Prop              | Type                      | Default       | Description                               |
52: | ----------------- | ------------------------- | ------------- | ----------------------------------------- |
53: | `defaultLabel`    | `string`                  | `"Watchlist"` | Initial text displayed in the chip        |
54: | `showThemeToggle` | `boolean`                 | -             | Whether to show a theme toggle (reserved) |
55: | `onChange`        | `(value: string) => void` | -             | Callback fired when the label is saved    |

(End of file - total 55 lines)
</content>

content.text:
Here are the results of my search. The core demo/preview component system with "Demo" and "Code" tabs is built from several interconnected files:

---

## Primary Files (the Demo/Code tab toggle system)

### 1. `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/components/preview-toggle.tsx`
**This is the central component that implements the "Demo" and "Code" tab toggle.** It uses a local `useState` to switch between two views:
- Renders two buttons labeled **"Demo"** and **"Code"**
- Tracks `view` state as `"demo" | "code"` (defaults to `"demo"`)
- Shows `demo` ReactNode when "Demo" is active, `code` ReactNode when "Code" is active
- Styling: active tab gets `text-foreground`, inactive gets `text-muted-foreground`

### 2. `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/components/component-preview.tsx`
**The main wrapper that builds the demo box and code view, then passes them to `PreviewToggle`.** It:
- Creates a **demo area** styled as a rounded border box with a subtle dot-grid background pattern, `min-h-[350px]`, centered content
- Creates a **code area** using `ComponentSource` (syntax-highlighted source code)
- Combines them via `<PreviewToggle demo={demo} code={code} />`
- Accepts `name`, `src`, `title`, `children`, and `className` props

### 3. `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/components/component-source.tsx`
**Renders the syntax-highlighted source code shown in the "Code" tab.** It:
- Fetches demo or registry source via `getDemoSource`/`getRegistrySource`
- Formats code with `formatCode` and highlights with `highlightCode`
- Renders a `<figure>` with optional `<figcaption>` title, a `<CopyButton>`, and highlighted HTML
- Can be collapsible or non-collapsible

### 4. `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/mdx-components.tsx`
**Registers `ComponentPreview` as an MDX component** (line 41), making it available in `.mdx` documentation pages. Also registers supporting components like `Tabs`, `TabsList`, `TabsTrigger`, `TabsContent`, `CodeTabs`, `ComponentSource`, etc.

---

## Supporting Files

### 5. `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/components/code-tabs.tsx`
A separate tabs component used for installation command switching (npm/yarn/pnpm/bun), not the Demo/Code toggle. Uses the `useConfig` hook to persist the selected installation type.

### 6. `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/components/ui/tabs.tsx`
The base `Tabs` UI primitives (Radix-based) with `Tabs`, `TabsList`, `TabsTrigger`, `TabsContent`. Includes haptic/sound feedback on tab switch via `useFeedback`.

---

## Demo Files (content rendered inside the "Demo" tab)

## MDX Pages that use `<ComponentPreview>` (26 pages)

All 13 component docs use the pattern:
```mdx
<ComponentPreview name="component-name">
  <DemoPreviewComponent />
</ComponentPreview>
```

Example from `/home/himmel/personal/prsui/content/docs/components/accordion.mdx`:
```mdx
import { BouncyAccordionPreview } from "@/demo/accordion-demo";

<ComponentPreview name="accordion">
  <BouncyAccordionPreview />
</ComponentPreview>
```

---

## Architecture Summary

The data flow is:

```
MDX page
  └─ <ComponentPreview name="..." children={DemoComponent}>
       ├─ demo box (styled container with dot-grid, renders children)
       ├─ code box (<ComponentSource> fetches + highlights source)
       └─ <PreviewToggle demo={demoBox} code={codeBox}>
            ├─ Button "Demo" → shows demo box
            └─ Button "Code" → shows code box
```
